channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ibercaja se hace con la plataforma tecnológica de Orange Bank España
item.description: Ibercaja ha firmado el acuerdo de adquisición de la plataforma tecnológica de Orange Bank España, con el objetivo de impulsar la transformación y el crecimiento del negocio de financiación al consumo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s gen AI launch is about global analysis
item.description: When Morgan Stanley announced its new generative AI support tools for financial advisors last week, it talked about gaining efficiencies from its notetaking abilities. But those who track the $54 billion financial firm said that its goal is much larger.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cybersecurity leadership for small businesses
item.description: Small businesses don't often have big cyber teams, but this this doesn't mean they aren’t getting attacked.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Sony Walkman at 45: Why Its Legacy Endures
item.description: Celebrating its 45th anniversary, the Sony Walkman's impact on music consumption and personal technology is still unmatched, with a legacy that transcends age.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Huawei succeeds where Microsoft failed miserably — HarmonyOS now on almost one billion devices, and China's largest mobile phone manufacturer has completely eliminated Android
item.description: Huawei’s mobile OS is on nearly a billion devices in China, and its new HarmonyOS Next removes all traces of Android.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams) , TechRadar

item.title: Top 5 Travel Destinations Most Prone to Online Booking Scams
item.description: Kurt Knutsson reveals the five travel destinations most frequently targeted by cybercriminals through online booking scams, providing essential information for travelers to stay safe and secure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abandoning doggy doo? DNA will sniff you out and expose you
item.description: Some apartment complexes and homeowners associations are requiring residents to submit DNA samples from their dogs to track who is responsible for piles of dog waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI-Generated Instagram Creators on Horizon: Meta Unveils 'Beta' Tag
item.description: Meta's latest announcement reveals plans to introduce AI-generated versions of popular Instagram creators, featuring a 'beta' tag after the creator's name.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: TSA launches 3D scanners to detect explosives
item.description: The new technology will give agents a 3D image that can be turned 360 degrees for enhanced threat protection.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Video assistant referees to smart rings: Here’s the soccer tech at Euro 2024
item.description: From smart-enabled match balls and artificial intelligence to cryogenic recovery chambers, soccer is being transformed by the cutting-edge technology available to players, coaches and officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: FCC proposes rule requiring carriers to unlock all cellphones
item.description: The FCC wants carriers to unlock cellphones within 60 days of purchase so users can shop different networks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: What is a passkey? Why Apple is betting on password-free tech
item.description: When iOS 18 debuts in September, Apple will bring myriad new security features to users. But the company will also debut a new API for app and website developers that will protect users in more fundamental way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Heartless scammers are preying on people's lost pet anguish
item.description: In a new "lost pet" scheme, scammers take advantage of upset pet owners searching for their lost companions in an attempt to get money through deception.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unlocking YouTube Video Insights: How to Search Transcripts on Your Mobile
item.description: Discover how to quickly locate specific information within long YouTube videos using Google's upcoming Gemini AI tool on Android devices, revolutionizing your video search experience.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pescadero Mansion Lists for $8 Million, Features Solarium and Indoor Pool
item.description: A luxurious 6,000-square-foot mansion in Pescadero has been listed for sale at $8 million, boasting a two-story solarium and an indoor swimming pool with access to an outdoor lap pool.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: With new AI tools available, State Department encourages experimentation
item.description: State wants employees to try out new AI tools like State Chat and North Star, but also share their own use cases to help drive the agency's AI approach. The post With new AI tools available, State Department encourages experimentation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google opens access to 2 million context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro
item.description: Google has opened access to the 2-million-token context window of the Gemini 1.5 Pro AI model. The company also is giving developers access to code execution capabilities in the Gemini API and making the Gemma 2 model available in Google AI Studio. These announcements were made on June 27.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Uber, Lyft agree to minimum pay, benefits in Massachusetts settlement
item.description: Uber and Lyft have agreed to offer a minimum wage, paid sick leave and other benefits for drivers as part of a $175 million settlement resolving a multi-year legal battle with Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple Intelligence Delayed Amid Anticompetitive Fears, EU Commissioner Claims
item.description: European Commission Vice President Margrethe Vestager criticizes Apple's decision to delay the introduction of its AI-powered Apple Intelligence, suggesting it may be an attempt to stifle competition
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Cloud’s Vertex AI gets new grounding options
item.description: Google Cloud is introducing a new set of grounding options that will further enable enterprises to reduce hallucinations across their generative AI-based applications and agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The perils of overengineering generative AI systems
item.description: Cloud is the easiest way to build generative AI systems; that’s why cloud revenues are skyrocketing. However, many of these systems are overengineered, which drives complexity and unnecessary costs. Overengineering is a familiar issue.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Box announces upgrade to Box AI, integration with GPT-4o
item.description: Box has unveiled a new set of features in Box AI that includes an integration with GPT-4o, support for image and spreadsheet files and the Box AI for Metadata API.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Doxxing victims in California may gain right to sue to ‘bring some power back’
item.description: A state bill would allow doxxing victims to sue those responsible in civil court for as much as $30,000, plus attorneys’ fees and court costs. Supporters say it’s needed to stem online harassment against LGBTQ residents and others.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: AI success: Real or hallucination?
item.description: Ever consider how much of AI is a hallucination? Not how much AI output is hallucination, but rather how much of what we hear or read about it is genuine? Could it be that AI success is mostly hype, and that all the hype may be hurting the technology rather than helping? Enterprises need to get to t
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Broadcom bolsters VMware Edge Compute Stack
item.description: A slew of updates in VMware Edge Compute Stack (ECS) 3.5 are aimed at helping customers more easily manage edge devices, applications, and infrastructure across multiple locations, and industry watchers say the updated platform will support AI at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NASA Astronauts Delayed at Space Station due to Boeing Starliner Issues
item.description: Astronauts are spending extra time at the International Space Station as NASA and Boeing work to resolve issues that arose during the capsule's journey, affecting its ability to reach the space station.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pure Storage adds AI features for security and performance
item.description: Pure Storage is upping its AI game with a pair of announcements, including a new storage-as-a-service offering that’s designed for AI workloads and new AI-driven security features to detect and prevent ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised its national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Record Labels Sue AI Music Generators Suno and Udio for Copyright Infringement on Works by Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey
item.description: Big record companies are suing AI music startups Suno and Udio, alleging copyright infringement through the exploitation of recorded works from iconic artists like Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chinese hackers have stepped up attacks on Taiwanese organizations, cybersecurity firm says
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A suspected Chinese state-sponsored hacking group has stepped up its targeting of Taiwanese organizations, particularly those in sectors such as government, education, technology and diplomacy, according to cybersecurity intelligence company Recorded Future.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5
